# Characterization fixture: a Go-produced (doltlite 0.10.8) `.autosk/db`

`db` is a real `.autosk/db` created by the **Go** `autosk` binary, which links
doltlite **0.10.8** (the Go-side pin — see the repo `Makefile`). It is a doltlite
container in **format v11** (`CTLD` header, format version `0x0b`).

## What it pins (the Phase 0 finding)

The spike DISPROVED the plan's forward-compat assumption (§2.1, §9, §10): a
Go-0.10.8 db is **NOT** readable under Rust/doltlite-0.11.8. doltlite 0.11.0 is a
documented breaking on-disk format change, and the break is mutual — 0.11.8
writes container **format v12** (`0x0c`) and refuses the older v11 container.

So this fixture is a **characterization** input, not a forward-compat input. The
test `tests/format_compat.rs` uses it to pin the actual, reproducible behaviour
so it cannot silently regress:

- `go_0_10_8_fixture_is_container_format_v11` — asserts this file's `CTLD`
  header reports format version 11.
- `go_0_10_8_db_is_not_readable_under_0_11_8` — asserts that opening this file
  under 0.11.8 (then forcing a read) fails with `SQLITE_NOTADB`. If a future
  doltlite bump makes this open SUCCEED, that test fails loudly — the signal to
  revisit the migration decision (ask-8037b4).

There is **no row-by-row comparison** of this db against any golden snapshot —
0.11.8 cannot open it, so its rows are never read. (See `tasks.golden` below.)

## `tasks.golden`

`tasks.golden` is TSV: `id<TAB>title<TAB>status<TAB>priority`, ordered by `id`.
It records the rows this fixture was created with, but **no test reads them back
out of `db`** — since the criterion-#2 pivot it serves only as the deterministic
SEED row set for the freshly created 0.11.8 dbs in `tests/common/mod.rs`
(`fresh_native_db`). It is seed data, not a snapshot verified against this
fixture; the two can drift without any test catching it.

## Provenance / regeneration

Generated by `scripts/make-forward-compat-fixture.sh` with the Go binary
(`make build`, doltlite 0.10.8). The task ids are random `ask-XXXXXX`, so the
script always writes `db` and `tasks.golden` together in one run. The db is a
single self-contained file (no WAL/journal sidecars).

Do not edit `db` by hand.
